template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — DEMAND LETTER**:
Professional demand letter addressed to the insurance company/adjuster (3-6 pages).
DO NOT generate a title like "DEMAND LETTER" at the top.
Start with date and recipient address.

STRUCTURE (follow this exactly):
1. Date line (use today's date provided above)
2. "VIA [method]" line (Certified Mail, Return Receipt Requested / Email)
3. Recipient: Insurance Company, Adjuster name, Address (each on its own line)
4. "Re:" block — multi-line format:
   Re:    Our Client:          [client name]
          Claim Number:        [claim/policy number]
          Date of Incident:    [date]
5. "Dear [Adjuster Name]:" — use actual name from case data
6. OPENING PARAGRAPH: State purpose — demand for full and fair compensation for injuries.
7. LIABILITY SECTION (heading: **Liability**): Brief factual narrative of the incident. Establish fault.
8. INJURIES & TREATMENT SECTION (heading: **Injuries and Medical Treatment**): Describe injuries, diagnoses (use ICD codes from case data), treatment timeline, providers, ongoing symptoms/prognosis.
9. DAMAGES SECTION (heading: **Damages**):
   - Past medical expenses (itemized by provider with amounts from case data)
   - Future medical expenses (if projected)
   - Lost wages
   - Pain and suffering (use multiplier or per diem from case data if available)
   - Total damages
10. DEMAND: State specific demand amount. Set 30-day response deadline.
11. CLOSING: "Very truly yours," — then STOP if stationery signature is applied.

USE ONLY REAL VALUES from the case data provided. Zero placeholders for names, dates, amounts.
INCLUDE a damages summary table if medical records provide specific amounts.
TONE: Professional, assertive, factual. Build the strongest case for maximum recovery.
hints: Key elements to address: liability theory, factual narrative, damages breakdown (medical, lost wages, pain/suffering), settlement demand amount, response deadline.
